extra.message: Avoid hardcoding values that are meant to be secret. Found a hardcoded string used in here.
Unsanitized input from the HTTP request body flows into yaml.load, where it is used to deserialize an object. This may result in an Unsafe Deserialization vulnerability.
Unsanitized input from the HTTP request body flows into execute, where it is used in an SQL query. This may result in an SQL Injection vulnerability.

Unsanitized input from the request URL flows into flask.render_template_string, where it is used to construct a template that gets rendered. This may result in a Server-Side Template Injection vulnerability.
Unsanitized input from the request URL flows into flask.render_template_string, where it is used to render an HTML page returned to the user. This may result in a Cross-Site Scripting attack (XSS).
Do not hardcode passwords in code. Found hardcoded password used in _.
Certificate verification is disabled by setting verify to False in requests.post. This may lead to Man-in-the-middle attacks.
Certificate verification is disabled by setting verify to False in requests.post. This may lead to Man-in-the-middle attacks.

Certificate verification is disabled by setting verify to False in requests.get. This may lead to Man-in-the-middle attacks.
The integrity check disable against the received token which might be tampered with.
hashlib.md5 is insecure. Consider changing it to a secure hashing algorithm.
Do not hardcode passwords in code. Found hardcoded password used in a dictionary key.
